focus_point: Whether the ArkUI page lifecycle hook onPageShow fires when returning to a page via router.back() (back-navigation), so a per-page refresh can re-snapshot shared singleton repository state
task_excerpt: Cross-page match-count sync: when the user adds/removes a pantry item on the Pantry page (or clears pantry on Settings, or toggles a favorite on a recipe card) and then navigates back, the Discover/Search/Saved/RecipeDetail pages must re-snapshot the shared repository singletons and recompute match counts. Plan mandates each consuming page call viewModel.refresh() in onPageShow.
project_evidence: Pages are @Entry @Component structs navigated via router.pushUrl/router.back() (HarmonyOS router API). Each page binds an @Observed ViewModel. There is no shared reactive store; instead plan introduces singleton repositories (PantryRepository/FavoritesRepository) whose snapshot() must be re-read on page re-entry. Need to confirm onPageShow is the correct lifecycle hook for re-entry refresh on router.back(), and whether aboutToAppear also fires on back (it should not).
extra_constraints: Offline only. Confirm onPageShow is invoked on router.back() re-entry to a previously-pushed page, and that aboutToAppear is NOT re-invoked on back. If onPageShow does not fire on back-nav, identify the correct alternative hook.
